title: Akhenaten, Nefertiti, and Three Daughters
topic_path: New Kingdom and Third Intermediate Period

assessment_items.question_content:
**Where is the god Aten in this sculptural relief?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6de5a6c05eeb655c2ea69a1bc74bc978.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The sun god Aten is represented by the circular disk, and the cobra inside signifies this is the supreme deity.

choices.content: The round disk in the center of the image
The male figure on the left of the composition
The child sitting in the lap of the woman
The small child on the woman's shoulder
The female figure on the left of the composition

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does this sculpture depart from the stylistic conventions of earlier Egyptian art?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6de5a6c05eeb655c2ea69a1bc74bc978.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Egyptian relief carvings often render the body in a composite view so that frontal and profile features appear at once. In other words, this does not break with earlier stylistic  conventions in the view of the body or in the format of the sunken relief carving.

Created during the Amarna period, this relief shows an intimate and relaxed scene of the royal family who appear informally posed and distorted, radically different from the conventions established in earlier Egyptian art.

choices.content: the anatomical distortions
the intimacy of the scene
the composite view of the body
the relaxed postures of the figures
the relief carving technique

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does this image reinforce the belief that Akhenaten and Nefertiti are Aten's only representatives on earth?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6de5a6c05eeb655c2ea69a1bc74bc978.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Hierarchical scale, which shows the relative importance of each figure in a scene, is not used to signal Akhenaten's relationship to Aten. The representation of the couple's daughters are also not known to reinforce the belief that Akhenaten and Nefertiti are Aten's only representatives on earth.

The rays of the sun (a symbol of Aten) and the ankhs radiating toward the two large figures, reinforce the belief that Akhenaten and Nefertiti are Aten's only representatives on earth.

choices.content: The rays of the sun (a symbol of Aten) and the ankhs radiate toward the two large figures.
The couple's many children
Nefertiti's throne is inscribed with a symbol of the god Aten.
The use of hierarchical scale

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the original function of this object?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6de5a6c05eeb655c2ea69a1bc74bc978.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sculpture would have been used as an altar and placed in a private home as a reminder of the king's relationship with Aten.

choices.content: It was an altar in a home.
It was an altar found in Nefertiti's burial chamber.
It was a decorative panel for a public space.
It was an altar found in Akhenaten's burial chamber.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What motivated the cultural changes that characterize Akhenaten's reign?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Akhenaten broke with traditions by rejecting the established polytheistic state religion around the god Amun and creating a new monotheistic culture devoted to the sun god Aten.

choices.content: A powerful priestly class emerged that assumed royal authority.
The king created a monotheistic state religion devoted to the god Aten.
The king and his family were exiled after a military coup.
A severe drought forced the king to relocate the Egyptian capital.
The king issued an edict sharing power with his wife Nefertiti.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best explains the distinctive Amarna style in Egyptian art?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: After almost 3000 years of continuity, Akhenaten broke with traditions by rejecting the established state religion and creating a new monotheistic culture around the sun god Aten.

choices.content: Akhenaten broke with the traditions and beliefs of the past and rejected the god Amun.
Akhenaten was in exile and unable to find highly skilled sculptors.
Akhenaten was a foreign ruler who imported the Amarna style from his own culture.
Akhenaten's own physical deformity influenced figurative representation during his reign.
